issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open function changes the visible state of the navigation drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu is now open, blocking access to navigation.
issuesFound.fix: In the `open` function, add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. In the `close` function, add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown is opened or closed via click or hover, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the expanded state of the dropdown, blocking access to its content.
issuesFound.fix: In `_openInstance`, add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`. In `_closeInstance`, add: `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons do not have aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (e.g., monthly/annual) change visual state but lack aria-pressed to indicate which is active. Screen reader users cannot perceive the selected billing period, blocking access to pricing information.
issuesFound.fix: In `_syncToggleState`, for each button in `_periodBtns`, set aria-pressed based on active state. Add: `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', isActive ? 'true' : 'false');` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors do not set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the error is displayed but aria-invalid="true" is not set on the input. Screen reader users are not alerted to the invalid state, blocking correction of errors.
issuesFound.fix: In the else block of `_renderResult` (when result.valid is false), add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`. In the valid block, add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an initial aria-expanded attribute when the page loads with the menu closed. Screen reader users may not know the initial state of the menu, degrading experience.
issuesFound.fix: In the `init` function, after setting `_initialised = true;`, add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` to indicate the menu is closed by default.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered without an initial aria-expanded attribute, even though they start closed. Screen reader users may not know the initial state, degrading experience.
issuesFound.fix: In the `register` function, after setting panel attributes, add: `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` to indicate the dropdown is closed initially.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation controller's open(), close(), and toggle() functions change the visible state of the drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users cannot perceive whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2080: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`. Line 2095: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing module's period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) change visual active state but do not set aria-pressed. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is selected, violating WCAG 4.1.2 for toggle button states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3405: replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates visually but no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the changing result count, degrading experience per WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2795: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory) : 'Filter applied');`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validator marks fields with error classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid inputs. Screen reader users are not alerted to field errors, violating WCAG 4.1.2 for form validation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3960: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true);` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`. Line 3955: add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false);` after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile nav trigger does not have aria-expanded initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button lacks an initial aria-expanded attribute when the page loads with the menu closed. Screen reader users may not know the control is collapsible, degrading experience per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2135: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_initialised = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not have aria-expanded initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are registered without an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute when panels are hidden. Screen reader users may not know these controls are expandable, degrading experience per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2245: add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after setting aria-haspopup.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle switch for billing period (monthly/annual) does not update the aria-checked attribute, preventing screen reader users from knowing the current state of the switch.
issuesFound.fix: In the _syncToggleState function of the pricing module, add: _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', period === 'year');

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is invalid, the validation function adds a CSS class but does not set aria-invalid="true", so screen readers are not informed of the error state.
issuesFound.fix: In the _renderResult function, in the else block (when result.valid is false), add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded set to false on page load when the drawer is closed, causing screen readers to miss the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav init function, after getting the trigger and drawer elements, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for dropdown menu triggers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded set to false on registration when the panel is hidden, causing screen readers to miss the initial state.
issuesFound.fix: In the dropdownMenu register function, after setting panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''), add: setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product filter updates not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied, the updated count of visible products is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: In the productFilter applyFilters function, after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches), add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Toggle function for mobile navigation does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav toggle function changes the visible state of the drawer but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button, breaking screen reader communication.
issuesFound.fix: In the mobileNav toggle function, call setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', _open) after toggling the _open state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function for dropdown menu does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close functions change the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button, breaking screen reader communication.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', true); In _closeInstance, add: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', false);

issuesFound.title: Toggle button for billing period does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button (switch) does not update aria-pressed to reflect the pressed state, so screen reader users cannot perceive the current selection.
issuesFound.fix: In the _onToggleClick function, after determining the next period, add: _toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-pressed', next === 'year');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update for search results not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When search results are fetched and rendered, the number of suggestions is not announced to screen readers via a live region, leaving users unaware of the update.
issuesFound.fix: In the _fetchAndRender function, after _updateStatus(results.length), add: liveRegion.announce(`${results.length} suggestion${results.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} available`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error for newsletter email does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When the newsletter email validation fails, the input field is not marked with aria-invalid="true", so screen readers are not informed of the error.
issuesFound.fix: In the newsletterForm _onSubmit function, in the validation failure block, add: _emailEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

Form validation error for newsletter email does not set aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid prop support
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an "invalid" prop for visual error styling but does not set aria-invalid="true" when invalid. Screen reader users are not informed of the error state programmatically.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid prop support

issuesFound.title: Select component missing aria-invalid prop support

issuesFound.title: FormGroup error message not connected via aria-describedby
issuesFound.explanation: When an error message is present, the FormGroup does not automatically link it to the form field via aria-describedby. Screen reader users may not hear the error description when focusing the field.
issuesFound.fix: Line 600: Pass errorId to children and ensure the form field receives aria-describedby. Update the component to accept an errorId prop and pass it to the child input. This requires changes to the FormGroup implementation and how it renders children.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) without providing any alternative visible focus style such as a box‑shadow or colored border. Keyboard‑only users lose a clear focus indicator on all focusable elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 274: :focus-visible { outline: none; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); /* add visible focus style, e.g.: box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); */ }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transition that animates `transform`, but no `prefers-reduced-motion` media query disables or alters this animation, violating users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 215: .btn { transition: ...; /* add reduced‑motion override */ }
  Line 274: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover (translateY)
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section moves with `transform: translateY(-1px)` on hover, but there is no reduced‑motion override to disable this movement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 629: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); /* add reduced‑motion override */ }
  Line 274: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img (transform transition)
issuesFound.explanation: Hero images animate `transform` (perspective/rotate) with a transition, yet no reduced‑motion media query disables this animation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 692: .hero-image img { transition: transform var(--transition-slower); /* add reduced‑motion override */ }
  Line 274: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover (transform)
issuesFound.explanation: Hovering the hero image triggers a `transform` animation, but no reduced‑motion override is provided.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 704: .hero-image img:hover { transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(0deg) rotateX(0deg); /* add reduced‑motion override */ }
  Line 274: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover (translateY & scale)

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em;`, which compresses spacing between words and can reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 166: body { word-spacing: normal; /* or a positive value */ }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual cue
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual differentiator (border, background, font‑weight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 184: a { text-decoration: underline; /* or add another visual style such as border-bottom */ }

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility (.sr-only) does not hide content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static; width: auto; height: auto;` and lacks the required `position: absolute; clip: rect(0,0,0,0);` pattern, meaning elements with this class remain visible to assistive technologies and defeat the purpose of screen‑reader‑only content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2365: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0,0,0,0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on global :focus-visible without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector removes the outline and does not provide any alternative visible focus style (e.g., box‑shadow or colored border). Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, affecting all interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Visually‑hidden utility (.sr-only) removes content from ATs
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which does **not** hide the element from visual layout but also does **not** hide it from assistive technologies. This defeats the purpose of a screen‑reader‑only technique and can expose hidden content to ATs unintentionally.
issuesFound.fix:
Replace the rule with a proper implementation, e.g.:  
Line 469: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0 0 0 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed globally without alternative visual cue
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing the default underline from all links. No other visual differentiator (color change, border, background) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual cues.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update `aria‑expanded` on state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` and `close()` functions of the `mobileNav` module change CSS classes and scroll‑lock but never modify the `aria-expanded` attribute on the toggle button, leaving the ARIA state out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix: In `open()` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close()` call `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion trigger buttons do not update `aria‑expanded`
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ items are built with a button (or element with role="button") that controls a panel, but the `open()` and `close()` functions only add/remove CSS classes and do not set `aria-expanded` on the trigger. Screen readers cannot perceive whether an answer is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: In `open(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true')`; in `close(item)` call `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger elements lack initial `aria‑expanded` attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The `register()` function of the `dropdownMenu` module adds `aria-haspopup="true"` but does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button. Without this, assistive technologies cannot know the default collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: After creating the `DropdownInstance`, set `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields (and does not clear it on valid fields)
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate()` function calls `srUtils.markInvalid()` which only adds `aria-describedby` but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the field, nor does it remove it on valid input. This violates WCAG 2.2 requirements for communicating validation errors to AT.
issuesFound.fix: In `validate(field)` after obtaining the `FieldValidationResult`, call `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', result.valid ? 'false' : 'true')`. Also ensure that on a valid result the attribute is removed or set to `"false"`.

issuesFound.title: `aria‑expanded` not initialised on page load for dropdown triggers (duplicate of Issue 4 but flagged for the initial page‑load state)
issuesFound.explanation: Because the initial `aria-expanded` attribute is missing, the dropdown’s collapsed state is not conveyed to AT until the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Same as Issue 4 – set `aria-expanded="false"` during registration.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In dropdownMenu, the _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions add or remove the “open” class on the panel but never modify aria‑expanded on the trigger button. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive the expanded/collapsed state of the dropdown.

issuesFound.title: Search input lacks an accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The site‑search input element receives ARIA attributes for autocomplete and live‑region linking, but no <label>, aria‑label, title or aria‑describedby is provided. Without an accessible name, screen‑reader users cannot understand the purpose of the field.
issuesFound.fix:
```js
_inputEl = qs(SELECTORS.searchInput);
…
setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-label', STRINGS.searchLabel || 'Site search'); // ← add this line
```

issuesFound.title: Product‑filter tab buttons do not reflect state via aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter‑tab buttons receive a visual “active” class, but their aria‑pressed state is never updated. Users of assistive technology cannot tell which filter is currently applied.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator._renderResult adds error‑message styling but never toggles the aria‑invalid attribute on the field. WCAG 4.1.2 requires that invalid form controls be programmatically indicated.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers lack initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are initialized, their trigger buttons are not given an explicit aria‑expanded="false". Without this, assistive technologies may assume the menu is already expanded.

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav trigger missing aria‑controls link to drawer
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button controls the navigation drawer but never receives an aria‑controls attribute referencing the drawer’s id, breaking the relationship required for screen‑reader users.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle buttons receive a visual “active” class but never expose their state via aria‑pressed, preventing assistive technology from conveying which period is selected.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu trigger buttons do not update aria‑expanded when panels open/close
issuesFound.explanation: In the dropdown menu component the trigger elements receive `aria‑haspopup` but never have their `aria‑expanded` attribute toggled. Users of assistive technology cannot know whether the submenu is visible.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria‑expanded state on page load

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle buttons lack aria‑pressed and aria‑controls attributes
issuesFound.explanation: The period selection buttons (`[data-period]`) are styled as toggle controls but never receive `aria‑pressed` to indicate the active state, nor do they reference the pricing panel via `aria‑controls`. This violates the ARIA button pattern for two‑state controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2100 (after selecting `_periodBtns`): `setAttr(btn, 'role', 'button');`

    Line 2110 (when creating each button, if a corresponding panel exists): `setAttr(btn, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_gridEl, 'pricing-grid'));`

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on fields with errors
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid visually but only adds an error message via `srUtils.markInvalid`, which does **not** set the required `aria‑invalid="true"` attribute. Screen readers therefore do not announce the error state.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not clear aria‑invalid when a field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: The `clearField` method calls `srUtils.markValid`, which removes the error message but does not remove the `aria‑invalid` attribute, leaving the field permanently marked as invalid to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Toggle widgets (mobile navigation, dropdown menus) do not initialise aria‑expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Both the mobile‑nav trigger and dropdown triggers are created without an initial `aria‑expanded="false"` attribute, so screen readers cannot infer the collapsed state before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix: (see Issues 2 and 4 for the exact lines to add `setAttr(..., 'aria-expanded', 'false');`).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. The correct implementation should use `position: absolute` and `clip: rect(0 0 0 0)`.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` selector applies a `transform` animation on hover, but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1666: `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: `body { word-spacing: normal; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a` selector removes underlines with `text-decoration: none`, and no alternative visual indicator (e.g., border-bottom, font-weight) is provided on `:hover` or `:focus`, relying solely on colour to distinguish links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 420: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or provide an alternative visual indicator on hover/focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative focus style on the same selector. This removes focus indicators for keyboard users, violating WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in the dropdownMenu controller opens a dropdown panel but does not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because screen readers will not be notified of the state change, leaving users unaware that the dropdown is now open.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12348: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`

---

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function toggles the billing period between monthly and annual but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because screen readers will not be notified of the state change, leaving users unaware of the current billing period.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 14215: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`

---

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updatePlanCard` function updates the displayed price, period label, and savings badge when the billing period changes, but does not announce these changes to screen readers via an ARIA live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) because users who rely on screen readers will not be aware of the updated pricing information.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10819: `liveRegion.announce(priceFormatter.format(periodPrice, plan.currency) + ' ' + priceFormatter.periodLabel(period));`

---

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_validateAll` function in the contactForm controller identifies invalid fields and renders error messages but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute to `true` on those fields. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because screen readers will not be notified that the field contains an error, leaving users unaware of validation issues.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 15815: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

---

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the inlineValidator controller clears the error message and removes the error class when a field becomes valid but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute to `false` or remove it. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 15710: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

---

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the dropdownMenu controller registers dropdown instances but does not initialise the `aria-expanded` attribute on trigger buttons for dropdowns that are closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) because screen readers will not have accurate initial state information for dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12240: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

---

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle button (`.pricing-toggle`) is a `<div>` with `role="switch"` and `tabindex="0"` but does not have a `keydown` event handler for Space or Enter keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) because keyboard users cannot activate the toggle using standard keys.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The dot indicators in the testimonials carousel are `<button>` elements with `role="tab"` but the carousel does not have a `keydown` event handler for Left Arrow, Right Arrow, Home, or End keys to navigate between dots. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) because keyboard users cannot navigate between carousel slides using standard keys.

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module uses a click handler (`_onTriggerClick`) on the nav toggle button but does not provide matching keyboard support (Enter/Space). Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot open/close the mobile menu.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module toggles the navigation menu but does not announce the state change (opened/closed) to screen readers. Users who rely on screen readers are unaware of the menu state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module marks fields as invalid by adding error classes and messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid fields. Screen readers do not announce the invalid state.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` module clears error messages and classes on valid input but does not reset `aria-invalid` to `false`. Screen readers continue to announce the field as invalid.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module initialises the mobile navigation but does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the nav toggle button at page load. Screen readers may not correctly announce the initial closed state.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.toggle()` function toggles the mobile menu state but does not update `aria-expanded` on the nav toggle button. Screen readers are unaware of the state change.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing` module's `_onToggleClick` function toggles the billing period but does not update `aria-pressed` on the toggle button. Screen readers do not announce the pressed state.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter` module updates the product count badge when filters are applied but does not announce the change to screen readers via a live region.

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard support

issuesFound.title: Missing keyboard handler for non-native interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onTriggerClick` (lines 1023-1040) handles click events on dropdown triggers but does not include a corresponding keyboard handler for Enter and Space keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard, as non-native interactive elements must be fully operable via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a replacement visible focus style (such as box-shadow or border). This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify which element is focused.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class exposes content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and clip: auto, which makes content visually visible on the page instead of hiding it. This defeats the purpose of screen-reader-only content and clutters the visual interface.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses #5588bb text on a #ffffff background (via variables), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1. This fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button

This removes the focus ring for all elements that do not have a specific utility class, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.

issuesFound.fix: Line 530: `height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the recommended 44×44 pixel size for the search submit button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 548: `width: 44px; height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44px. These toggle buttons control content filtering and should have larger touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 916: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on pricing toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44px. This toggle switches between monthly and annual billing views.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1017: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on product card buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .product-body .btn-secondary sets min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44px. These call-to-action buttons within product cards should have larger touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1110: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px, below the recommended 44px. Social media links in the footer should meet enhanced target size guidelines.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1289: `min-height: 44px;`

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Screen-reader-only text will be visible to all users, breaking the visual layout and potentially exposing content meant only for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 314: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause spatial movement. The empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 368 does not override this transition, potentially causing vestibular issues for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 368: Add `.btn { transition: none; }` inside the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes transform in its transition (transition: transform var(--transition-slower)...). The @media block at line 811 is empty and does not disable this transition, which can trigger motion sickness.
issuesFound.fix: Line 811: Add `.hero-image img { transition: none; }` and `.hero-image img:hover { transform: none; }` inside the @media block.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words below normal. This can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities, and can cause words to visually merge together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 285: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` or set to `word-spacing: normal;`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class sets background-color: var(--colour-error) (#ee9999) with color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 1.6:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for both normal text (4.5:1) and large text (3:1). Users with low vision will not be able to read the button text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 653: `.btn-danger { background-color: var(--colour-error-dark); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }` (using #991b1b which provides ~7:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on statistics values
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-value selector uses white text (var(--colour-neutral-0)) on a gradient background that includes #bb88ee (light purple). The contrast ratio between white and #bb88ee is approximately 2.6:1, which fails the 3:1 requirement for large text (48px/36pt qualifies as large). Users will have difficulty reading the statistics.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1039: Add a text-shadow or change the gradient to ensure at least 3:1 contrast, or use a darker text color such as `color: var(--colour-neutral-900);` with a semi-transparent background.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on statistics labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label selector uses rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (30% opacity white) on a gradient background including light purple (#bb88ee). The resulting contrast ratio is well below 3:1, making the labels illegible for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1043: `.stat-label { color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); }` or `color: var(--colour-neutral-0);`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on newsletter paragraph text
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .newsletter-section > p sets color: rgba(255,255,255,0.8) on a gradient background containing #bb88ee. The effective contrast ratio is approximately 2.35:1, failing WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1).
issuesFound.fix: Line 1192: `.newsletter-section > p { color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }` or ensure background is darkened to provide 4.5:1 contrast.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. While some specific links (feature cards, footer) add underlines on hover, body text links rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 269: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor;` to links within prose content.

issuesFound.title: Body text font size below 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, making the root em unit 12px. Since body inherits this via var(--font-size-base) (1rem), the base text size is 12px, which is below the 16px (1rem) recommended for readability and can cause text to appear too small for users with mild visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 280: `font-size: 16px;` or `font-size: 100%;` (assuming browser default 16px)

This removes the default focus ring from all interactive elements, making it impossible for keyboard users to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer nav links

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button has a transition on the transform property that creates motion when activated, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 336: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a transform transition to slide into view, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable this animation for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 303: (add after existing rules) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card has a transform animation on hover (translateY and scale) that is not covered by the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query, which only targets the base .pricing-card:hover.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1132: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover, .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia.
issuesFound.fix: Line 228: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels

issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section paragraph uses rgba(255,255,255,0.8) (80% white) on a blue-purple gradient background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.4:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (16px).

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The module declares a `_trap` variable intended for focus trap management but never initializes it when the menu opens. Keyboard focus is not trapped within the mobile navigation drawer when open, allowing users to tab out of the menu into the background page content, violating WCAG focus management requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in open(): After `_lockScroll();` add:
  `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers do not update aria-expanded when panels toggle
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown panels are opened or closed via the _openInstance and _closeInstance functions, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated. This prevents screen reader users from knowing whether the dropdown is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _openInstance: After `addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);` add:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`
  Line in _closeInstance: After `removeClass(inst.panel, CSS.open);` add:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in markInvalid: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add:
  `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors from a field, the function removes the error message reference from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false", leaving the field incorrectly marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in markValid: Before the closing brace of the function, add:
  `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce results changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates visually but the change is not announced to screen reader users via a live region, leaving them unaware of how many results are now displayed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _updateCountBadge: After `_countBadge.textContent = String(count);` add:
  `liveRegion.announce(\`\${count} product\${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found\`);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load, so screen readers do not know the initial collapsed state of the menu before interaction.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in init: After `if (!_trigger || !_drawer) return;` add:
  `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when registered, so screen readers do not know the initial collapsed state of the dropdown panels.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in register: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add:
  `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is not initialized with aria-expanded="false", so screen readers do not know the initial collapsed state of the suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in mount: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');` add:
  `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions toggle the visual state but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the expanded/collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `_open = true;`. Line mobileNav.close(): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close instance functions do not set aria-expanded on the trigger element, preventing screen readers from announcing the state of the dropdown panel.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the start of the function. Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance(): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the start of the function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the code sets the panel to visible but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies when opening.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes but do not update the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing._syncToggleState(): Inside the `_periodBtns.forEach` loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown or hidden, the visual update is not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that the content has changed or how many results are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters(): After `emit(_gridEl, 'filter:applied', ...)` add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price updates visually but the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod(): After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...)` add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items changes visible content but does not announce the state change or the newly visible content to screen readers via the live region utility.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open(): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} expanded`);` before the emit call. Line faqAccordion.close(): Add `liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} collapsed`);` before the emit call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself, which is required to communicate the invalid state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid(): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid removal
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is cleared or marked as valid, the function removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute (or set it to "false"), potentially leaving the field marked as invalid for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid(): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after removing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the drawer is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init(): After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);` and the null check, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialisation
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set, but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" for the collapsed initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu.register(): After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');` add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progressbar missing aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element is assigned role="progressbar" and receives aria-valuenow updates, but aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax are not set in the JavaScript initialisation, leaving the progress value without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line multiStepWizard.init(): After `_progressBar = qs('[data-wizard-progress]', _form);` add `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');` and `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');` (or use `_steps.length` for max if representing steps).

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle element is given role="switch" and tabindex="0" when it is not a native button, but no aria-label or aria-labelledby is provided to give it an accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.init(): After setting the role and tabindex on `_toggleEl`, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-label', 'Switch billing period');`.

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button is selected from the DOM but the code does not ensure it has an accessible name (aria-label) if the HTML text content is missing or insufficient.
issuesFound.fix: Line scrollToTop.init(): After `_btn = qs(SELECTORS.scrollTopBtn);` and the null check, add `if (!_btn.getAttribute('aria-label')) setAttr(_btn, 'aria-label', STRINGS.labels.scrollToTop || 'Scroll to top');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close not returning focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the focus trap is deactivated but focus is not programmatically returned to the trigger button that opened the menu, violating the requirement to return focus to the triggering element on dialog closure.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.close(): At the end of the function (after emit), add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`.

fpTitles: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialisation
multiStepWizard progressbar missing aria-valuemin/aria-valuemax

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions in dropdownMenu show and hide the dropdown panels but fail to set aria-expanded="true" or "false" on the trigger buttons. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3020: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`
  Line 3040: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions animate the answer panels but do not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the question triggers, despite the triggers having the attribute initially set. This breaks the accordion pattern for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4860: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` inside open() after `item.open = true;`
  Line 4890: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside close() after `item.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function updates the visual active state of filter tab buttons but does not set aria-pressed to indicate which filter is currently active. This is required for toggle buttons that select one option from a set.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4375: Replace the forEach body with:
  `const isActive = btn.getAttribute('data-filter-value') === _productFilterState.activeCategory; toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState() function in the pricing module updates the active billing period button styles but fails to set aria-pressed="true" on the active button and "false" on inactive ones. This prevents screen readers from announcing the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4655: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);` inside the forEach after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function adds the error message reference via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot identify that the field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2285: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` inside markInvalid after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description reference but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false", leaving stale invalid states on fields that have been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2315: Add `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` inside markValid after clearing the describedby references.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function queries the trigger button but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on page load, violating the requirement that toggle buttons reflect their initial closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2825: Add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` at the end of the init function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register() function sets up new dropdown triggers but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" on the trigger element, leaving the initial state ambiguous to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3095: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside register() after `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init() function configures the search input as a combobox but does not initialise aria-expanded="false" to indicate the suggestion list is initially closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4155: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` inside init() after mounting the listbox.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function activates the navigation drawer but does not activate the focus trap (createFocusTrap is imported but never instantiated or activated). Keyboard users can tab outside the drawer onto the underlying page, violating focus containment requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2842: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` 
  Line 2843: Add `_trap.activate();` 
  Line 2844: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');` (combine with Issue 1 fix)

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product cards and count badges but does not announce the filter results (e.g., "Showing 5 products") via a live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4295: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Showing ${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''}\`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions accept an announceToSR parameter but never use it to announce the expanded/collapsed state to screen readers via the liveRegion utility.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4875: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(\`${item.question} \${item.open ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}\`);` inside open()
  Line 4905: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(\`${item.question} \${item.open ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}\`);` inside close()

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates price values visually but does not announce the billing period change or new prices to screen readers, preventing users from knowing the prices have updated when they toggle between monthly and annual.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4645: Add `liveRegion.announce(\`Prices updated to \${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing\`);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav accordion triggers missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that expand and collapse mobile navigation sections have aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute. This prevents assistive technology from announcing the expanded state of the submenus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 760: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The disclosure buttons controlling FAQ answer panels have aria-controls but lack aria-expanded. Screen readers cannot convey whether each answer is currently visible or hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: <button type="button" id={btnId} aria-controls={panelId} aria-expanded={isOpen} className="faq-item__trigger" onClick={() => onToggle(item.id)}>

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button is an icon-only control rendered by IconButton, but no aria-label prop is provided. The button has no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 820: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in the mobile navigation panel is an IconButton without an aria-label prop. Screen readers cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 755: <IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous button is an IconButton without an aria-label prop, leaving screen reader users without context for the control's function.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1360: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel next button is an IconButton without an aria-label prop, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1370: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" but FormGroup is not provided an errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1470: <FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.error} error={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: ContactForm name input references non-existent error element

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without non-colour visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The a { text-decoration: none; } rule removes underlines from all links. For body text links not inside navigation or component-specific contexts, there is no non-colour visual differentiator (no border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight) to distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false" on page load. Assistive technologies cannot report the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2758: add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown, the trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized to "false". Assistive technologies cannot report the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2918: add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` after the line that sets aria-controls.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to link the field to its error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2292: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description reference but never clears aria-invalid. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2299: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` after the isElement check.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not set aria-checked for role="switch"
issuesFound.explanation: The billing toggle element is assigned role="switch" but aria-checked is never set or updated. For role="switch", aria-checked is required to indicate the on/off state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5200: add `_toggleEl.setAttribute('aria-checked', 'false');` when initializing, and in _onToggleClick add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', String(next === 'year'));` after setting the period.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot announce the expanded state of the navigation menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2691: After `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not update aria-expanded on trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function hides the drawer but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce the collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2702: After `_open = false;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared as null and never assigned a focus trap via createFocusTrap(). When the mobile nav opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, and when it closes, focus is not returned to the trigger element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: After `_initialised = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { escapeDeactivates: true, onDeactivate: close });`. Line 2695: After `_lockScroll();`, add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`. Line 2715: After `_unlockScroll();`, add `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function shows the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2834: After `inst.open = true;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not set aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance() function hides the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2847: After `inst.open = false;`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register() does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, aria-expanded is never initialized to "false" for closed dropdowns. Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2928: After `panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function expands the FAQ panel but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4480: After `item.open = true;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function collapses the FAQ panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4512: After `item.open = false;`, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers cannot announce that the field has an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1948: After `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid() function removes the aria-describedby reference but never clears aria-invalid. Fields remain marked as invalid even after errors are resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1967: After the aria-describedby cleanup, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() does not set aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function toggles CSS classes but never sets aria-pressed on filter tab buttons. Screen readers cannot announce which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3840: Replace `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` with `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel.init() does not initialize aria-pressed on play button
issuesFound.explanation: The play/pause button's aria-pressed state is only set when play() or pause() is called, but not initialized on page load. Screen readers may not know the initial playing state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5125: After `goTo(0);`, add `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() does not announce filter changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When products are filtered and visibility changes, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the product list has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3805: After `_updateEmptyState(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches === productCardRegistry.count() ? 'all' : s.activeCategory) || 'Products filtered');`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() does not announce price changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period changes and prices update, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4205: After `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.pricingAnnual || 'Switched to annual billing') : (STRINGS.pricingMonthly || 'Switched to monthly billing'));`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips.render() does not announce chip changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed, no live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed of the current active filters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3975: After `chips.forEach(c => _containerEl.appendChild(_makeChip(c.label, c.key, c.value)));`, add `if (chips.length) liveRegion.announce(chips.length === 1 ? '1 filter applied' : chips.length + ' filters applied');`

faqAccordion.close() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
