issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not move focus into the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, focus is not moved to the first focusable element inside the drawer. Keyboard users must manually Tab into the drawer, and screen reader users may not realize the drawer content is available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2694: Add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();` before the emit statement.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not return focus to the trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the focus order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2713: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();` after `_unlockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() does not return focus to trigger when requested
issuesFound.explanation: The returnFocus parameter is accepted but never used. When closing a dropdown via Escape or outside click, focus should optionally return to the trigger button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2870: Add `if (returnFocus && inst.trigger) inst.trigger.focus();` before the emit statement.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop for accessibility, but ThemeToggle does not pass one. The button becomes an icon-only button without an accessible name, making it unusable for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1020: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle"> {ICONS[theme]} </IconButton>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal placed on overlay instead of dialog
issuesFound.explanation: aria-modal="true" is placed on the modal-overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". This violates ARIA specification and may cause inconsistent screen reader behavior.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2050: <div className="modal-overlay" onClick={onClose}> <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav missing accessible name on repeated nav landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple navigation landmarks (SkipLinks nav, DesktopNav nav, MobileNav nav, footer navs). The DesktopNav nav element has no aria-label or aria-labelledby, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish between navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~870: <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: MobileNav body missing accessible name on repeated nav landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple navigation landmarks. The MobileNav nav element has no aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish between navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~990: <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks nav missing accessible name on repeated nav landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple navigation landmarks. The SkipLinks nav element has no aria-label, making it difficult for screen reader users to distinguish between navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~500: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation links">

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the button is in a loading state (isLoading=true), aria-busy is not set. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is in a busy/loading state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~590: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Spinner decorative ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner__ring span is a decorative visual element but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce meaningless content for the decorative ring.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~620: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: StatItem value missing accessible label for formatted numbers
issuesFound.explanation: The stat.value displays formatted numbers like "40,000+" but the Stat interface provides valueLabel ("40,000 plus") for screen readers. The valueLabel is never used, so screen reader users hear the ambiguous visual representation.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~1150:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible (isVisible=false) lack aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may navigate to off-screen content that is not visually perceivable.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1650: <div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: When item.current is true, the link receives a visual "current" style but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify the current page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~880: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: When item.current is true, the link receives a visual "current" style but lacks aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify the current page link.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1000: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk (*) for required fields is not hidden from screen readers. The VisuallyHidden "(required)" text provides the accessible label, but the asterisk itself is also announced, creating redundant information.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~680: *

fpTitles: MobileNav body missing accessible name on repeated nav landmark

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. Screen reader users hear nothing or only the emoji icon character (☀️, 🌙, or 💻), making the button's purpose completely unclear.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section toggle buttons missing aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The expandable section buttons in the mobile navigation have aria-controls but lack aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot tell whether each navigation section is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link uses only a CSS class (nav-link--current) to indicate the active state. Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in mobile navigation uses only a CSS class to indicate the active state. Screen reader users cannot programmatically identify which link represents the current page.

issuesFound.title: Statistic values not accessible to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The stat values like "40,000+" and "99.9%" may be mispronounced or unclear to screen reader users. The data includes a valueLabel property ("40,000 plus", "99.9 percent") that is not exposed to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 913-916:

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The skip links navigation region has no aria-label or aria-labelledby. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot distinguish this nav from others when navigating by landmark.

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The desktop navigation region has no aria-label. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot easily distinguish this nav when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 664:
  <nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation body region missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation body region has no aria-label. With multiple navigation landmarks on the page, screen reader users cannot easily distinguish this nav when navigating by landmark.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 727:
  <nav className="mobile-nav__body" aria-label="Mobile navigation">

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The comment indicates the cloned list is for visual looping only and should be aria-hidden, but the attribute is not applied. Screen reader users will encounter duplicate content.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 974-984: {/* Aria-hidden clone for visual looping only. */}
    {INTEGRATIONS.map((item) => (
  • {item.name}
    • ))}

issuesFound.title: Spinner decorative ring not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner's visual ring element is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce the spinner element redundantly alongside the accessible label.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 437:
  <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span inside the Button component is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". While VisuallyHidden provides the accessible loading text, the decorative spinner element should be explicitly hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 391:
  {isLoading && (
    <>
      <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />
      <VisuallyHidden>{loadingLabel}</VisuallyHidden>
    </>
  )}

issuesFound.title: StarRating visual stars not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The inner span containing star characters (★ ☆) duplicates the accessible name already provided by the outer span's aria-label. Screen readers may announce the stars redundantly.

fpTitles: Desktop navigation region missing accessible name
Mobile navigation body region missing accessible name

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component's label element does not have the htmlFor attribute, so the label is not programmatically associated with its corresponding input. Screen reader users cannot click the label to focus the input, and the input's accessible name may not be properly announced.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~640: `<label htmlFor={htmlFor} className="form-label">`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle missing aria-label on IconButton
issuesFound.explanation: The ThemeToggle component renders an IconButton without passing the required aria-label prop. The icon content is hidden from assistive technology, leaving the button with no accessible name.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~930: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle" > {ICONS[theme]} </IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm error message not connected to input
issuesFound.explanation: The NewsletterForm passes an error prop to FormGroup that doesn't exist in the interface, and doesn't pass errorId. The TextInput's aria-describedby references 'newsletter-email-error' which doesn't exist in the DOM. Screen reader users won't hear validation error messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~2140: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: ContactForm error messages not connected to inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The ContactForm passes error props to FormGroup that don't exist in the interface, and doesn't pass errorId. Each TextInput's aria-describedby references IDs that don't exist. Screen reader users won't hear validation error messages for any field.
issuesFound.fix:
Line ~2270: `<FormGroup label="Full name" htmlFor="contact-name" required errorId="contact-name-error" errorMessage={name.state.touched ? name.state.error : undefined} ...>`
  Fix: Similar changes for email, subject, and message fields with corresponding errorId props.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component has isLoading state but doesn't communicate this to assistive technology via aria-busy. Screen reader users won't be informed that the button is in a loading/busy state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~610: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav renders links with a 'nav-link--current' class for the current page but doesn't include aria-current="page". Screen reader users won't be informed which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~960: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} >`

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The video play/pause toggle button conveys state through aria-label text changes but lacks aria-pressed. Screen reader users benefit from having both the state property and descriptive label.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1125: `<Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} ...>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button has aria-controls and a dynamic aria-label but is missing aria-expanded. Adding aria-expanded provides a consistent programmatic way for assistive technology to understand the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1080: `<button ... aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>`

issuesFound.title: Spinner ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner's visual ring element doesn't have aria-hidden="true". While there's a VisuallyHidden label, the decorative ring element should be explicitly hidden from assistive technology to prevent potential duplicate announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~685: `<span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel hidden slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Non-visible carousel slides don't have aria-hidden="true". All slides remain in the accessibility tree, so screen reader users may navigate through hidden content that isn't visually displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1880: `<div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible} ...>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron icons missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icons (▲/▼) in NavDropdown are decorative indicators of expanded state but lack aria-hidden="true". The state should be conveyed by aria-expanded, making the visual chevron redundant for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~900: ` {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'} `

issuesFound.title: Required field marker asterisk missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The visual asterisk (*) indicating required fields is announced by screen readers as "star" or similar, creating redundant information since "(required)" is already provided in VisuallyHidden. The asterisk should be hidden from assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~645: ` *`
